part.name: HOUSING
title_block_notes: Drawing title read as HOUSING in lower-right title block.
Metric units inferred from drawing dimensions and notes using mm, mm/min, kN, and Nm.
Title block material and default tolerance fields are not reliably legible in the raster image.
For any detail not shown refer to 3D model.

coordinate_system.primary_axis: +Z normal to the main front/primary view
coordinate_system.orientation_notes: Main front view is treated as the +Z face. X is left-to-right on the main view, Y is vertical on the main view, and Z is through the part as exposed by SECTION A-A and SECTION B-B. Access directions are assigned from this convention.
coordinate_system.datum_origin: Datum A is interpreted from the four A1-A4 circular datum target pads; exact coordinate origin is not explicitly shown.

features.name: Complex cast housing body
features.description: One-piece irregular housing/bracket body with central annular housing, four threaded mounting bosses, clevis/lug areas, ribs, pockets/windows, and controlled perimeter surfaces.

geometry.position_description: Overall complex housing; length from SECTION B-B dimensions 168.69 + 167.09, width from main-view maximum 232.62, max profile from Ø143.60 I/P in SECTION A-A.

dimensional_tolerances.dimension_type: overall length, derived from two sectional dimensions

evidence.cited_text: Overall irregular housing geometry visible across all views

features.name: Datum target pad pattern A1-A4
features.description: Four circular datum target pad/spotface areas around the M10 threaded mounting locations, identified as A1, A2, A3, and A4. These collectively establish datum A.

features.name: 4X M10 x 1.5 threaded mounting holes
features.description: Four internal threaded holes in the mounting bosses. Callout states M10 x 1.5 - 6H thread after coating. Depth is not explicitly legible; the holes are associated with the four datum target pads and insert/nut features.

geometry.position_description: Four threaded features at A1-A4 mounting boss locations on the main view.

features.name: 4X insert/nut counterbore or recesses

geometry.position_description: Coaxial with the four M10 threaded insert/nut locations; shown in SECTION D-D.

features.material_condition: nuts must remain flush or below bracket surface

dimensional_tolerances.dimension_type: insert/nut recess depth or axial dimension

datum_scheme.datum_hierarchy_notes: Primary datum A appears to be a datum-target plane from A1-A4 pads. Datums B and C are interpreted as the two Ø16.90 locating bores. Datum D is a cylindrical bore in SECTION A-A. Datum E is visible but not fully legible; it is tentatively mapped to the Ø74.00 coaxial step. Section-cut labels A-A, B-B, C-C, and D-D were treated as section identifiers, not datums.

feature_graph.graph_summary: The drawing depicts a complex cast housing/bracket named HOUSING with a large central annular/bore structure, radial stiffening ribs, irregular lightening pockets, four M10x1.5-6H threaded mounting-boss locations with Ø40 datum target pads A1-A4, two Ø16.90 locating/datum bores B and C, a coaxial central/sectional bore stack including Ø72.04 datum D, Ø70.00, Ø74.00, Ø82.00/82.71, Ø90.02, and a large Ø143.60 annular profile. The part also includes clevis/lug geometry with Ø30.60, Ø20.10, and 2X Ø10.50 holes, U-shaped clevis slots 55.20 mm wide, controlled inner/outer clevis faces, stepped lug walls, and multiple radii/chamfers/fillets. Primary datum A is interpreted from four pad targets, B and C from the two Ø16.90 bores, and D from the Ø72.04 bore. Several GD&T profile/position/flatness controls are legible but some values and datum references remain low-confidence due to raster legibility.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the material grade/specification from the title block.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material is required for downstream quoting, weight estimation, and interpretation of casting/coating requirements; the raster title-block material field is not legible enough to extract safely.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the general tolerance standard/default tolerances shown in the title block, if any.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Many dimensions have no explicit ± tolerance; the default drawing tolerance controls these dimensions.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the M10 threaded insert/hole depth and whether the four M10 holes are blind, through, or insert-nut features only; also confirm the counterbore/recess diameter associated with 4X 17.50.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The thread callout is clear but the depth and recess diameter are not; these affect complete 3D reconstruction of the mounting bosses.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as threaded insert/nut features with no modeled thread depth until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Please confirm through-depth/wall-thickness values for the Ø16.90 datum bores and clevis holes if they are not simply through the local wall thickness shown in sections.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Hole features are identified, but local material thickness is not always explicitly dimensioned at each hole axis.
clarifications.suggested_default: Model as through holes/through slots through the local lug or wall thickness visible in section.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the exact GD&T feature-control-frame values and datum references for the M10 pattern, Ø16.90 bores, Ø72.04 bore, Ø74/Ø30/Ø20 features, and clevis holes.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Several FCFs are present but small; extracted tolerances are low-confidence and may affect inspection datum relationships.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use the extracted best-read values with low confidence until verified.

clarifications.question: Please confirm whether the irregular pockets/windows and radial ribs are controlled solely by the 3D model/profile controls, or whether additional dimensions exist not readable in the raster.
clarifications.why_it_matters: These large cast features are visible but not fully dimensioned on the 2D sheet; complete 3D reconstruction needs their boundaries/depths.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat them as cast/profile-controlled geometry per 3D model and visible drawing outline.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the meaning of abbreviations O/R, I/P, U/R, A/C, and DRFT MAX on the drawing and whether those dimensions are finished dimensions, over-reference profile dimensions, or casting/draft controls.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The central diameter stack and several section dimensions use these abbreviations; exact interpretation affects feature classification and tolerance status.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat O/R and I/P values as dimensional references and DRFT MAX as draft control until confirmed.

clarifications.question: What is the depth of F021 (Cast lightening pockets/windows on main face)? No depth dimension was found on the drawing for this feature.
Please verify feature F014 (Draft/taper surfaces in central section). It was extracted with low confidence (0.64). Confirm whether this feature exists and its dimensions are correct.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Low-confidence features may be misread dimensions or hallucinated geometry. Agent 2 needs accurate data to plan machining operations.

guardrail_results.message: Title block and notes were reviewed first; title and units were extracted, but material and default tolerances were not legible enough for reliable extraction.

Envelope uses maximum visible dimensions from section/main views: 335.78 derived from 168.69+167.09, 232.62 main-view span, and Ø143.60 largest profile; no hole-count callout was misused as envelope width.
Thread scan found only one explicit metric thread callout: 4X M10X1.5-6H THREAD AFTER COATING. It is represented by F003.

Major hole callouts with Ø symbols were captured: M10 thread pattern, Ø16.90 B/C bores, Ø72.04 bore, Ø70/74/82/90 central stack, Ø30.60, Ø20.10, and 2X Ø10.50 clevis holes. Some counts/depths remain uncertain due to section legibility.

Explicit 4X 17.50 recess depth was captured for F004. Through/depth conditions for several bores/slots were inferred from section geometry but exact local depths are not fully dimensioned, so clarifications were added.
Section cuts A-A, B-B, C-C, D-D were separated from datum symbols. Datum A/B/C/D were identified with moderate confidence; datum E is low-confidence due to legibility.
Each feature has at least one physical or datum relationship; symmetric coaxial/parallel relationships were created in both directions where used.

No feature-specific Ra/surface-finish symbol was confidently visible. General deburr/flash/marking/coating notes were kept at part level instead of being applied as feature surface_finish requirements.

analysis_trace.summary: Processed one cropped contrast-enhanced drawing sheet. Main front/profile view is on the upper-left; sections and details surround it. Metric notation is used throughout.
issues: Some title-block fields and small GD&T frames remain difficult to read at raster resolution.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified main front view, SECTION A-A, SECTION B-B, SECTION C-C, SECTION D-D, DETAIL X, DETAIL Y, lower clevis detail views, lower-left notes, lower-right test table, and lower-right title block.
issues: Section letters A-A/B-B/C-C/D-D are cutting-plane identifiers and were not treated as datums.

analysis_trace.summary: Swept visible views and extracted body envelope, M10 thread pattern, datum pads, Ø16.90 datum bores, central coaxial bore/diameter stack, clevis holes/slots/faces, stepped wall geometry, perimeter surfaces, ribs, pockets, radii, chamfers, and fillets.
issues: Irregular cast pockets/ribs are visually clear but not dimensioned in detail on the 2D sheet.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated the central circular feature in the main view with the stepped coaxial diameters in SECTION A-A/B-B, and correlated clevis holes/slots across SECTION A-A, SECTION C-C, and lower detail views.
issues: Some O/R, I/P, U/R, A/C abbreviations are not fully interpreted.

analysis_trace.summary: Assembled feature graph with physical relationships: M10 threads to pads/recesses, coaxial central bore train, clevis holes to lug faces, pockets/ribs to body, and perimeter radii/chamfers/fillets to parent edges.
issues: Some relationships are inferred from visible section alignment rather than fully dimensioned coordinates.

analysis_trace.summary: Embedded limit dimensions and visible GD&T controls directly on feature nodes. Limit dimensions were converted to midpoint nominal values with plus/minus deviations. Profile, position, and flatness controls were attached to constrained features when legible.
issues: Several feature-control-frame values/datum references have confidence below 0.75 and are flagged for clarification.

analysis_trace.summary: Interpreted datum A from the four Ø40 A1-A4 target pads, datum B and C from left/right Ø16.90 bores, datum D from the Ø72.04 bore in SECTION A-A, and tentative datum E near the Ø74 central step.
issues: Datum E and some B/C GD&T references are low-confidence due to small text.

analysis_trace.summary: Captured part-level coating/thread-after-coating notes, deburr/flash restriction, marking note, restricted-substance note, nut protrusion note, and test requirements including nut push-out, anti-rotate torque, durability block test, extreme jounce/rebound/joints tests.
issues: Material, heat treatment, and default tolerance standard could not be reliably extracted from the title block.

analysis_trace.summary: Ran thread completeness, hole completeness, depth, dimension reread, relationship completeness, datum disambiguation, and envelope sanity checks. Confirmed only M10 thread callout was visible; confirmed Ø symbol was present before creating hole features; verified no random 2X/3X ordinate/count text was treated as a hole diameter without Ø.
issues: Depths for several through features remain inferred; exact GD&T and material/tolerance fields need confirmation.
